item.title: UNHCR Canada Private Partnerships and Philanthropy Internship 2026 in Toronto: Fully Paid Opportunity for Students and Recent Graduates
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Are you a student or recent graduate looking to gain practical experience in fundraising, donor relations, communications, philanthropy, marketing, and humanitarian work? The United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) Canada is offering an exciting opportunity through its Private Partnerships and Philanthropy (PPH) Internship Program 2026 in Toronto, Canada. This full-time internship provides aspiring professionals […]

The post UNHCR Canada Private Partnerships and Philanthropy Internship 2026 in Toronto: Fully Paid Opportunity for Students and Recent Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a student or recent graduate looking to gain practical experience in fundraising, donor relations, communications, philanthropy, marketing, and humanitarian work? The United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) Canada is offering an exciting opportunity through its Private Partnerships and Philanthropy (PPH) Internship Program 2026 in Toronto, Canada.

This full-time internship provides aspiring professionals with the chance to work directly with one of the world’s most respected humanitarian organizations while contributing to initiatives that support refugees and forcibly displaced people around the globe.

The internship offers hands-on exposure to fundraising operations, donor stewardship, event management, communications, research, database administration, and analytics. Successful candidates will work alongside experienced fundraising professionals and gain valuable real-world experience in the non-profit and international development sectors.

Application Deadline: June 25, 2026

About UNHCR

The United Nations Refugee Agency (UNHCR) is the leading global organization dedicated to protecting and assisting people forced to flee their homes because of conflict, violence, persecution, and humanitarian crises.

Established in 1950 following World War II, UNHCR has grown into a worldwide humanitarian network operating in more than 135 countries. The organization has helped millions of displaced individuals rebuild their lives and has been awarded the Nobel Peace Prize twice for its humanitarian contributions.

Today, UNHCR supports more than 80 million displaced people worldwide by providing:

  • Emergency shelter
  • Food assistance
  • Clean water
  • Protection services
  • Legal support
  • Access to education
  • Healthcare services
  • Advocacy for human rights and dignity

The Canadian office forms part of this global network and plays an important role in fundraising, partnerships, donor engagement, and public awareness initiatives.

Internship Overview

Position Details

  • Position Title: Private Partnerships and Philanthropy Intern
  • Organization: UNHCR Canada
  • Location: Toronto, Canada
  • Employment Type: Full-Time Internship
  • Start Date: July 1, 2026
  • Work Arrangement: On-site (Remote work not available)
  • Working Hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM
  • Hardship Level: H (No Hardship)
  • Family Status: Family Duty Station
  • Application Deadline: June 25, 2026

This internship is designed to immerse participants in the daily operations of a professional fundraising team while allowing them to make meaningful contributions to humanitarian efforts worldwide.

What Interns Will Learn

Participants will gain practical knowledge and professional skills in multiple areas, including:

  1. Donor communications
  2. Fundraising strategy
  3. Major donor cultivation
  4. Philanthropic partnerships
  5. Event planning and coordination
  6. Prospect research
  7. Relationship management
  8. Database administration
  9. Marketing analytics
  10. Stewardship practices
  11. Non-profit operations
  12. Humanitarian fundraising

Interns will also observe how mid-level and major donors are engaged through:

  • In-person meetings
  • Fundraising events
  • Email communications
  • Direct mail campaigns
  • Impact reporting initiatives

Key Responsibilities

1. Event Support

Interns will assist with organizing and managing donor and fundraising events.

Responsibilities may include:

  • Coordinating event logistics
  • Supporting venue setup
  • Managing schedules and timelines
  • Communicating with vendors
  • Preparing invitations
  • Coordinating catering arrangements
  • Organizing equipment and event materials

2. Donor Stewardship Activities

Building strong donor relationships is a critical part of fundraising success.

Interns will help coordinate donor stewardship activities such as:

  • Handwritten thank-you cards
  • Donor appreciation calls
  • Impact reports
  • Personalized communications
  • Event invitations
  • Relationship-building initiatives

3. Communications Support

Interns will support the development of communication materials for donors and stakeholders.

Tasks may include:

  • Project management support
  • Proposal preparation
  • Cover letter development
  • Thank-you letter creation
  • Editing communications
  • Fact-checking materials
  • Reviewing fundraising documents

4. Writing and Content Development

The internship offers significant exposure to professional writing.

Interns will:

  • Conduct background research
  • Gather supporting information
  • Draft written materials
  • Edit documents
  • Proofread communications
  • Assist with storytelling initiatives

5. Prospect Research

Participants will gain experience identifying and researching potential donors.

Responsibilities include:

  • Creating donor briefing notes
  • Researching donor interests
  • Preparing prospect profiles
  • Conducting due diligence support
  • Analyzing donor engagement opportunities

6. Database Management

Interns will work with donor management systems and databases.

Tasks include:

  • Updating donor records
  • Maintaining database accuracy
  • Recording donations
  • Tracking fundraising activities
  • Managing donor information

7. Analytics and Reporting

Data-driven fundraising is increasingly important in modern philanthropy.

Interns will assist in:

  • Collecting fundraising data
  • Analyzing campaign performance
  • Measuring donor engagement
  • Evaluating communication effectiveness
  • Producing reports for decision-making

8. Administrative Support

Additional responsibilities may include:

  • General office administration
  • Document preparation
  • Team support activities
  • Scheduling assistance
  • Special projects assigned by supervisors

Financial Benefits

UNHCR provides financial support for interns who do not receive funding from another source.

Monthly Internship Allowance

Eligible interns may receive:

  • Up to CAD $2,168.88 per month

This allowance is intended to help cover:

  • Food expenses
  • Local transportation costs

This financial support can significantly reduce the cost burden associated with living and working in Toronto during the internship period.

Why You Should Apply

This internship offers a unique combination of professional development and humanitarian impact.

Benefits include:

Professional Experience

  • Work with a globally respected UN organization.
  • Gain practical fundraising experience.
  • Develop project management skills.
  • Learn donor engagement strategies.

Career Development

  • Build a strong professional network.
  • Work alongside experienced fundraising professionals.
  • Enhance communication and writing abilities.
  • Strengthen research and analytical skills.

Humanitarian Impact

  • Contribute to refugee protection efforts.
  • Support life-saving humanitarian programs.
  • Help strengthen donor relationships that fund critical services worldwide.

Resume Enhancement

Experience with UNHCR is highly regarded across sectors including:

  • International development
  • Non-profit organizations
  • Public affairs
  • Communications
  • Philanthropy
  • Fundraising
  • Government relations
  • Corporate social responsibility

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet all eligibility requirements at the time of application.

Work Authorization Requirements

Candidates must be legally authorized to work in Canada and possess one of the following:

  • Canadian citizenship
  • Permanent Resident status with valid documentation
  • Valid Canadian work permit
  • Other written authorization under Canada’s Immigration and Refugee Protection Act

Educational Requirements

Applicants must satisfy one of the following:

  • Currently pursuing a university degree and have completed at least two years of study
  • Currently pursuing a college diploma and have completed at least two years of study
  • Graduated within the previous 24 months

Preferred Fields of Study

The following academic backgrounds are particularly advantageous:

  • Communications
  • Marketing
  • International Development
  • Related disciplines

Required Skills and Competencies

UNHCR seeks candidates who demonstrate:

Communication Skills

  • Strong written communication
  • Strong verbal communication
  • Professional correspondence abilities

Organizational Skills

  • Excellent attention to detail
  • Ability to manage multiple tasks
  • Strong time-management skills

Technical Skills

  • Microsoft Word proficiency
  • Microsoft Excel proficiency
  • Microsoft PowerPoint proficiency

Personal Qualities

  • Ability to work independently
  • Strong teamwork skills
  • Reliability and professionalism
  • Ability to perform under deadlines

Additional Assets

The following qualifications are considered advantageous:

  • Database management experience
  • Data entry experience
  • Fundraising experience
  • French language proficiency

Language Requirements

Required

  • Fluency in spoken English
  • Fluency in written English

Preferred

  • French language skills

Candidates who can communicate effectively in both English and French may have an additional advantage.

Diversity and Inclusion

UNHCR is committed to creating an inclusive workplace that reflects the diversity of the communities it serves.

Applications are encouraged from qualified individuals regardless of:

  • Race
  • Colour
  • National origin
  • Sex
  • Religion
  • Age
  • Disability
  • Sexual orientation
  • Gender identity

The organization also provides accommodations and recruitment support for applicants with disabilities whenever possible.

Important Application Information

Applicants should note the following:

  • UNHCR does not charge any fees at any stage of recruitment.
  • Applications are accepted only through the official UNHCR recruitment platform.
  • Applications sent by email will not be reviewed.
  • Candidates should beware of recruitment scams and fraudulent job offers.

How to Apply

Click here for more and to apply

Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official UNHCR online recruitment platform before the application deadline.

Deadline

June 25, 2026

Early applications are strongly encouraged as candidates will be evaluated based on their qualifications, education, skills, and suitability for the role.

For students and recent graduates interested in philanthropy, fundraising, communications, donor relations, and humanitarian work, the UNHCR Canada Private Partnerships and Philanthropy Internship 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity to gain professional experience while contributing to a global mission that changes lives.

Click here for more global internship opportunities

The post UNHCR Canada Private Partnerships and Philanthropy Internship 2026 in Toronto: Fully Paid Opportunity for Students and Recent Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Yale University Research Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded Predoctoral and Research Associate Opportunities in Development, Gender, and Experimental Economics
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
Yale University’s Inclusion Economics initiative has announced multiple exceptional opportunities for aspiring economists, researchers, and future PhD candidates through its highly competitive Predoctoral Fellowship and Research Associate programs. These positions offer unparalleled exposure to cutting-edge research in development economics, gender economics, behavioral economics, experimental economics, governance, financial inclusion, and public policy. Hosted through Yale Inclusion […]

The post Yale University Research Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded Predoctoral and Research Associate Opportunities in Development, Gender, and Experimental Economics appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Yale University’s Inclusion Economics initiative has announced multiple exceptional opportunities for aspiring economists, researchers, and future PhD candidates through its highly competitive Predoctoral Fellowship and Research Associate programs. These positions offer unparalleled exposure to cutting-edge research in development economics, gender economics, behavioral economics, experimental economics, governance, financial inclusion, and public policy.

Hosted through Yale Inclusion Economics (YIE), the Yale Economic Growth Center (EGC), and the MacMillan Center for International and Area Studies, these opportunities are designed for highly motivated individuals who want to build strong research careers while working directly with some of the world’s leading economists.

Successful candidates will gain hands-on experience in policy-relevant research projects spanning Kenya, India, Nepal, and other regions where economic inclusion, gender equality, governance reforms, and sustainable development remain critical priorities.

About Yale Inclusion Economics

Yale Inclusion Economics (YIE) is a globally recognized policy-focused research initiative that combines economics, political science, public policy, and interdisciplinary research to understand how governments, institutions, and communities can build more inclusive and accountable societies.

The initiative collaborates with researchers from Yale University and international partners, including:

  • Inclusion Economics India Centre
  • Inclusion Economics Nepal
  • International development organizations
  • Government agencies
  • Research institutions worldwide

Its core research areas include:

  • Gender and economic empowerment
  • Labor economics
  • Digital inclusion
  • Financial inclusion
  • Political economy and governance
  • Environmental economics
  • Experimental and behavioral economics
  • Public service delivery
  • Climate resilience and adaptation

Two Outstanding Opportunities Under One Research Ecosystem

Yale Inclusion Economics is recruiting for two closely related positions that together provide a unique pathway into high-impact economic research.

1. Development and Experimental Economics Research Fellow

Under the supervision of Professor Rohini Pande and Dr. Maria Kogelnik, this fellowship focuses on development economics, behavioral economics, and experimental research.

The selected fellow will contribute to innovative studies addressing real-world policy challenges across South Asia and East Africa.

Current research themes include:

  • Increasing women’s financial autonomy through national workfare programs
  • Improving labor market outcomes for women
  • Enhancing climate resilience through effective flood-warning systems
  • Improving government service delivery
  • Understanding political transitions and governance reforms
  • Environmental conservation through payment-for-ecosystem-services programs
  • Reducing gender gaps in mobile technology access
  • Expanding financial inclusion opportunities
  • Increasing economic participation among marginalized populations
  • Behavioral economics experiments conducted both online and in the field

The fellow will also support Yale’s growing Behavioral and Experimental Economics initiative through lab-based and field-based research activities.

To learn more about this position and apply, Click Here

2. Research Associate / Predoctoral Fellow – Kenya Research Portfolio

The second opportunity focuses specifically on applied economic research in Kenya and East Africa.

Working alongside Professor Rohini Pande, Professor Simone Schaner, Professor Charity Troyer-Moore, and Erik Jorgensen, the selected candidate will support major research initiatives aimed at empowering women in digital economies.

Key research themes include:

  • Women’s digital inclusion
  • Gender-responsive technology policies
  • Digital financial services
  • Economic empowerment programs
  • Gender-intentional policy design
  • Technology adoption among underserved populations
  • Evidence-based policy development

This position provides extensive exposure to the design, implementation, testing, monitoring, and evaluation of development interventions in East Africa.

What Makes These Opportunities Exceptional?

Work Directly with World-Leading Economists

Successful candidates will collaborate with internationally renowned scholars whose research influences policy decisions globally.

Faculty leadership includes:

  • Professor Rohini Pande
  • Dr. Maria Kogelnik
  • Professor Simone Schaner
  • Professor Charity Troyer-Moore
  • Erik Jorgensen

This level of mentorship is rare and can significantly strengthen future graduate school and academic applications.

Exposure to Cutting-Edge Research

Researchers will gain practical experience in:

  • Experimental economics
  • Behavioral economics
  • Development economics
  • Impact evaluation
  • Randomized controlled trials
  • Survey design
  • Data analytics
  • Policy research
  • Field experimentation
  • Public policy evaluation

Strong Preparation for PhD Programs

Both positions are specifically designed for individuals considering doctoral studies.

Participants will develop:

  • Research design skills
  • Advanced econometric skills
  • Academic writing expertise
  • Publication-oriented research experience
  • Professional research networks

These experiences make the positions highly attractive stepping stones toward PhD programs at leading universities worldwide.

Key Responsibilities

Successful candidates may be involved in a broad range of activities, including:

Data Management and Analysis

  • Cleaning large datasets
  • Managing administrative data
  • Organizing survey datasets
  • Monitoring intervention data
  • Conducting econometric analyses
  • Performing statistical modeling

Research Support

  • Literature reviews
  • Academic background research
  • Drafting grant proposals
  • Preparing policy briefs
  • Supporting manuscript development
  • Assisting with publication preparation

Field Research

  • Coordinating with research teams
  • Supporting survey implementation
  • Monitoring project activities
  • Working with implementation partners
  • Conducting field visits

Reporting and Communication

  • Preparing presentations
  • Drafting project reports
  • Creating slide decks
  • Developing research protocols
  • Contributing to knowledge-sharing initiatives

Skills and Qualifications Sought by Yale

Competitive candidates should possess:

Academic Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree or Master’s degree in:
    • Economics
    • Data Science
    • Statistics
    • International Development
    • Public Policy
    • Mathematics
    • Related quantitative disciplines

Technical Skills

  • Strong proficiency in Stata
  • Experience with R
  • Knowledge of Python is advantageous
  • Statistical analysis capabilities
  • Data management expertise

Personal Attributes

  • Strong academic record
  • Excellent communication skills
  • Independent problem-solving ability
  • Research curiosity
  • Attention to detail
  • Interest in development policy

International Travel Opportunities

One of the most exciting aspects of these positions is the opportunity to engage directly with field research activities.

Researchers may travel to:

  • Kenya
  • Nepal
  • India
  • East African study sites
  • South Asian research locations
  • Rural communities participating in research programs

This field experience provides invaluable insight into development challenges and policy implementation.

Career Benefits

These Yale opportunities offer significant professional advantages:

  1. Direct mentorship from internationally recognized economists.
  2. Experience at one of the world’s most prestigious universities.
  3. Preparation for competitive PhD applications.
  4. Exposure to real-world policy challenges.
  5. Advanced quantitative and analytical skill development.
  6. International research experience.
  7. Access to global academic networks.
  8. Opportunities for publication-oriented research.
  9. Experience working on high-impact development projects.

Application Process

Applicants are expected to submit:

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Cover Letter
  • Academic Transcripts
  • Additional application materials as requested

For the Development and Experimental Economics Research Fellow position, applicants must also complete a supplementary screening questionnaire as part of the application process.

Application Deadline

No fixed application deadline has been announced.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis.

Interested candidates are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible because positions may be filled before applications close.

Apply Here

To learn more, Click Here

Why You Should Apply

For aspiring economists, future PhD students, policy researchers, and development practitioners, these Yale Inclusion Economics opportunities provide an exceptional platform for professional growth. Participants will work on research that directly influences public policy, gender equality initiatives, financial inclusion strategies, climate resilience efforts, and economic development programs across multiple countries.

Few early-career opportunities offer the combination of elite mentorship, rigorous research training, international field experience, and direct policy relevance available through Yale Inclusion Economics. Candidates seeking to build impactful careers in academia, public policy, international development, or applied economics should strongly consider applying.

Discover more global research fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Yale University Research Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded Predoctoral and Research Associate Opportunities in Development, Gender, and Experimental Economics appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GovAI Winter Fellowships 2027: Fully Funded AI Governance Research Fellowships in London and Washington, DC with £12,000 Salary or $21,000 Stipend
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming governments, economies, industries, and societies around the world. As policymakers, researchers, and institutions grapple with the opportunities and challenges presented by advanced AI systems, the need for skilled professionals capable of shaping effective AI governance frameworks has never been greater. To help meet this growing demand, the Centre for the […]

The post GovAI Winter Fellowships 2027: Fully Funded AI Governance Research Fellowships in London and Washington, DC with £12,000 Salary or $21,000 Stipend appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming governments, economies, industries, and societies around the world. As policymakers, researchers, and institutions grapple with the opportunities and challenges presented by advanced AI systems, the need for skilled professionals capable of shaping effective AI governance frameworks has never been greater.

To help meet this growing demand, the Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI) has opened applications for two prestigious fellowship programs: the GovAI UK Winter Fellowship 2027 (Research Track) and the GovAI DC Winter Fellowship 2027.

These highly competitive three-month fellowships are designed to help aspiring and experienced researchers, policy professionals, academics, and emerging technology specialists launch or accelerate impactful careers in AI governance and public policy.

Both fellowships offer participants the opportunity to conduct independent research, receive mentorship from leading experts, engage with influential policy networks, and develop expertise in one of the world’s fastest-growing policy fields.

Successful fellows will join GovAI in either London, United Kingdom, or Washington, DC, United States, where they will work full-time on research projects that contribute to understanding and improving governance for advanced artificial intelligence systems.

Applications for both programs are currently open, with a deadline of 12 July 2026.

About GovAI

The Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI) is one of the world’s leading organizations dedicated to understanding and improving governance approaches for advanced artificial intelligence.

Founded at Yale University in 2016, GovAI was among the first academic organizations to systematically study the policy implications of AI progress.

Since publishing its influential research agenda in 2018, GovAI has helped define the emerging field of AI governance and continues to provide research and policy guidance to governments, companies, and civil society organizations worldwide.

Today, GovAI operates as an independent nonprofit think tank focused on helping decision-makers navigate the transition toward a future shaped by increasingly capable AI systems.

Its researchers and affiliates possess expertise in:

  • AI governance
  • Frontier AI regulation
  • AI economics
  • Risk analysis
  • Threat modeling
  • AI forecasting
  • Technical governance
  • Agent governance
  • International cooperation
  • US-China relations
  • Cybersecurity
  • Biosecurity
  • AI safety

GovAI researchers have published work in internationally recognized venues including:

  • Science
  • Nature
  • NeurIPS
  • FAccT

Why GovAI Fellowships Are Highly Respected

One reason these fellowships are particularly attractive is the strength of GovAI’s professional network.

GovAI alumni have secured influential roles in:

Governments

  • White House Office of Science and Technology Policy
  • National Security Council
  • U.S. Senate
  • Department of Homeland Security
  • Department of Commerce
  • UK Government
  • European Union institutions

Leading AI Companies

  • OpenAI
  • Anthropic
  • Google DeepMind

Think Tanks

  • RAND
  • Center for Security and Emerging Technology (CSET)
  • Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)
  • Center for a New American Security (CNAS)
  • Foundation for American Innovation

Universities

  • Yale University
  • Stanford University
  • Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
  • University of Oxford
  • University of Cambridge

For individuals seeking long-term careers in AI governance, technology policy, national security, regulation, or public policy, these fellowships provide exceptional exposure and networking opportunities.

Understanding the Two Fellowship Tracks

Although both fellowships share similar goals, they focus on different policy ecosystems and geographic contexts.

GovAI UK Winter Fellowship 2027 (Research Track)

The UK Fellowship is designed primarily for individuals interested in global AI governance and policy research.

Participants conduct independent research while building expertise across international AI governance challenges.

Fellowship Duration

18 January 2027 – 9 April 2027

Location

London, United Kingdom

Financial Support

Selected fellows receive:

  • £12,000 salary
  • Travel support to London
  • Temporary work visa sponsorship
  • Office workspace
  • Weekday lunches

Focus Areas

The UK fellowship supports research related to:

  • Global AI governance
  • AI regulation
  • AI forecasting
  • Risk management
  • International cooperation
  • Agent governance
  • Technical governance
  • Frontier AI policy
  • AI economics
  • Emerging technology governance

Learn More Here

GovAI DC Winter Fellowship 2027

The Washington, DC fellowship focuses specifically on AI policymaking within the United States political system.

The program emphasizes:

  • Bipartisan engagement
  • Policy relevance
  • Practical governance solutions
  • Understanding how AI policy is shaped in the United States

Fellowship Duration

18 January 2027 – 9 April 2027

Location

Washington, DC, United States

Financial Support

Selected fellows receive:

  • $21,000 stipend
  • Travel support to Washington, DC
  • Office workspace
  • Weekday lunches

Important Eligibility Note

Applicants must already possess the legal right to work in the United States.

GovAI does not sponsor visas for the DC Fellowship.

Focus Areas

The fellowship supports research and policy analysis connected to:

  • U.S. AI policy
  • Emerging technology governance
  • National security and AI
  • AI regulation
  • Technology competition
  • International technology policy
  • Economic impacts of AI
  • AI innovation and governance

Learn More Here

What Fellows Will Do

Participants in both fellowships engage in a structured yet flexible research experience.

Independent Research Projects

Each fellow conducts a research project of their choice.

Potential outputs include:

  • Research reports
  • White papers
  • Academic papers
  • Journal articles
  • Policy briefs
  • Op-eds
  • Public-facing analyses
  • Blog posts

Dedicated Mentorship

Each fellow is paired with a supervisor from GovAI’s team or network.

Supervisors provide:

  • Research guidance
  • Project feedback
  • Career mentorship
  • Publication advice
  • Strategic direction

AI Governance Exploration Phase

During the first two weeks, fellows explore:

  • Current policy debates
  • Governance challenges
  • Research gaps
  • Emerging issues
  • Priority policy questions

They then refine their research proposal before beginning full project work.

Professional Development Opportunities

Both fellowships offer extensive training and professional development.

Participants gain access to:

Expert Q&A Sessions

Engage with leading voices in:

  • AI governance
  • Public policy
  • National security
  • Technology regulation
  • Economics
  • International relations

Weekly Seminars

Seminars deepen understanding of:

  • AI policy frameworks
  • Governance challenges
  • Risk management approaches
  • Regulatory developments

Skills Workshops

Workshops help fellows strengthen:

  • Policy writing
  • Research methodology
  • Communication skills
  • Strategic analysis
  • Stakeholder engagement

Peer Feedback Sessions

Work-in-progress meetings allow fellows to:

  • Share ideas
  • Receive feedback
  • Improve research quality
  • Collaborate with peers

Networking Events

Participants build relationships with:

  • Policymakers
  • Researchers
  • Academics
  • Technology leaders
  • Think tank experts

Who Should Apply?

GovAI encourages applications from a broad range of professional and academic backgrounds.

Ideal candidates may include:

Researchers and Academics

  • PhD students
  • Postdoctoral researchers
  • Faculty members
  • Independent scholars

Policy Professionals

  • Government officials
  • Policy advisors
  • Legislative staff
  • Public sector analysts

Technology Professionals

  • Engineers
  • AI researchers
  • Product specialists
  • Technology strategists

Civil Society Leaders

  • Advocacy professionals
  • NGO practitioners
  • Public-interest researchers

Students

Both programs welcome strong candidates at earlier career stages, including undergraduates and graduate students.

Relevant Academic and Professional Backgrounds

Applicants may come from fields including:

  • Public Policy
  • Political Science
  • Economics
  • Computer Science
  • Engineering
  • Biosecurity
  • Cybersecurity
  • International Relations
  • China Studies
  • Risk Management
  • Law
  • Governance
  • Technology Policy

Importantly, there are no strict degree requirements.

Selection Criteria

Applicants are evaluated based on:

Quality of Work

Demonstrated ability to produce:

  • High-quality analysis
  • Well-reasoned arguments
  • Clear writing
  • Insightful research

Relevant Expertise

Knowledge or experience that may contribute to AI governance research.

Judgment

Ability to:

  • Prioritize effectively
  • Evaluate opportunities
  • Assess policy relevance
  • Make sound decisions

Team Fit

Candidates should demonstrate:

  • Intellectual honesty
  • Curiosity
  • Collaboration
  • Openness to feedback
  • Professional integrity

Application Process

The application process is identical for both fellowships.

Stage 1: Written Application

Applicants submit:

  • Written application materials
  • Background information
  • Experience details

Selected candidates complete a 20-minute abstract reasoning assessment.

Stage 2: Paid Remote Work Test

Invitations issued during August 2026.

Stage 3: Interview and Reference Checks

Conducted remotely during September 2026.

Final Decisions

Expected in October 2026.

Click HERE to apply for the GovAI UK Winter Fellowship 2027, Research Track.

Click HERE to apply for the GovAI DC Winter Fellowship 2027.

Important Deadline

Application Deadline:

12 July 2026

UK Fellowship:
23:59 BST

DC Fellowship:
11:59 PM ET

Why These Fellowships Matter

As artificial intelligence continues to reshape global systems, governments and institutions urgently need professionals capable of understanding and governing advanced AI technologies.

The GovAI Winter Fellowships provide participants with:

  • Financial support
  • Mentorship
  • Professional development
  • Policy exposure
  • Global networking
  • Research opportunities
  • Career acceleration

Few opportunities combine these elements at such a high level.

Conclusion

The GovAI UK Winter Fellowship 2027 and GovAI DC Winter Fellowship 2027 represent two of the most prestigious opportunities available for individuals interested in artificial intelligence governance, technology policy, public policy, and emerging technology research. Whether applicants are interested in global AI governance from London or U.S.-focused AI policy from Washington, DC, both fellowships offer unparalleled access to expert mentorship, policy networks, independent research opportunities, and meaningful career development.

With generous financial support, world-class training, and direct engagement with leaders shaping the future of AI governance, these programs provide an exceptional pathway for researchers, students, policy professionals, and technology experts seeking to contribute to one of the most important policy challenges of our time. Interested candidates should submit their applications before the 12 July 2026 deadline.

Discover more global Fellowship, Job and AI opportunities on Opportunities for Youth (OFY).

The post GovAI Winter Fellowships 2027: Fully Funded AI Governance Research Fellowships in London and Washington, DC with £12,000 Salary or $21,000 Stipend appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–27 at the University of Oxford: Fully Funded Three-Year Research Opportunity for Early Career Scholars
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Faculty of Asian and Middle Eastern Studies (AMES) at the University of Oxford has officially opened applications for expressions of interest for the prestigious British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–27. This highly competitive fellowship provides an exceptional opportunity for outstanding early-career researchers in the Humanities and Social Sciences to advance their academic careers within one […]

The post British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–27 at the University of Oxford: Fully Funded Three-Year Research Opportunity for Early Career Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Faculty of Asian and Middle Eastern Studies (AMES) at the University of Oxford has officially opened applications for expressions of interest for the prestigious British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–27. This highly competitive fellowship provides an exceptional opportunity for outstanding early-career researchers in the Humanities and Social Sciences to advance their academic careers within one of the world’s leading universities.

The British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowship scheme is designed to support promising scholars who have recently completed their doctoral studies or are nearing completion. Through this three-year fellowship, successful candidates will gain valuable experience in both research and teaching while developing a strong academic profile that can significantly enhance their prospects for securing permanent academic positions in the future.

About the British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowship

The British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowship is one of the United Kingdom’s most respected funding schemes for early-career researchers. It aims to provide outstanding scholars with the time, resources, and institutional support necessary to pursue innovative research projects while gaining valuable university-level teaching experience.

Hosted through the Faculty of Asian and Middle Eastern Studies at the University of Oxford, the fellowship offers scholars the opportunity to work within a world-renowned academic environment known for excellence in research, teaching, and interdisciplinary collaboration.

The fellowship specifically supports scholars working within:

  • Humanities disciplines
  • Social Science disciplines
  • Interdisciplinary research projects that align with the British Academy’s objectives
  • Research related to Asian, Middle Eastern, and associated studies where appropriate

Why Apply for the British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowship?

The fellowship offers a unique platform for early-career researchers to establish themselves within academia and build a strong foundation for long-term success.

Key benefits include:

1. Three Years of Dedicated Research Support

Successful fellows receive the opportunity to focus extensively on their research agenda over a three-year period, allowing them to develop substantial scholarly outputs and advance their academic expertise.

2. Teaching Experience in a World-Class Institution

The fellowship enables scholars to gain practical teaching experience within a university environment, helping them develop essential academic skills that are highly valued for future faculty positions.

3. Enhanced Career Development

The scheme is specifically designed to strengthen participants’ research and teaching portfolios, improving their competitiveness for permanent academic appointments at leading universities worldwide.

4. Access to Oxford’s Academic Community

Fellows become part of one of the world’s most prestigious academic communities, benefiting from:

  • International research networks
  • Academic seminars and conferences
  • Interdisciplinary collaborations
  • Access to exceptional libraries and research resources
  • Mentorship from leading scholars in their field

5. Professional Mentorship

Applicants are required to identify a mentor within the Faculty of Asian and Middle Eastern Studies. This mentorship relationship provides valuable academic guidance, professional support, and opportunities for scholarly development throughout the fellowship process.

Expression of Interest Process at Oxford

The Faculty of Asian and Middle Eastern Studies has established an internal selection process for candidates wishing to apply through Oxford.

Interested scholars must first submit an expression of interest to the Faculty before proceeding to the British Academy application stage.

Required Application Materials

Applicants should prepare and submit:

  1. A completed application form.
  2. Details of their proposed research project.
  3. Supporting information demonstrating academic excellence and suitability for the fellowship.
  4. Any additional materials requested by the Faculty.

Mentor Requirement

An important component of the application process is securing a prospective mentor within the Faculty.

Applicants must:

  • Identify a suitable mentor within the Faculty of Asian and Middle Eastern Studies.
  • Obtain the mentor’s agreement to support the application.
  • Ensure the mentor submits an email confirming their willingness to serve in this role.
  • Have the mentor provide a brief assessment of the proposed application.

It is important to note that while applicants must secure mentor agreement before the deadline, mentors are not expected to contribute directly to the preparation of applications at this initial stage.

Additional Circumstances Statement

The Faculty recognizes that personal or professional circumstances may affect an individual’s research trajectory.

Applicants may submit a separate statement outlining circumstances that may have impacted their research record, including but not limited to:

  • Illness
  • Parental leave
  • Caring responsibilities
  • Other significant personal circumstances

Providing such information allows the Faculty to consider research achievements within the appropriate context.

Selection and Next Steps

Applications received by the Faculty will undergo internal review.

Candidates selected for institutional support will be contacted by the University’s Research Facilitation team and provided with further information regarding:

  • Application procedures
  • Submission requirements
  • Fellowship timelines
  • British Academy application system access
  • Additional documentation requirements

Only candidates selected through Oxford’s internal process will proceed to the next stage of the fellowship competition.

Important Deadline

Expression of Interest Submission Deadline

Date: Wednesday, 1 July 2026

Time: 17:00 (5:00 PM)

Applicants must ensure that:

  • Their application form is submitted by the deadline.
  • Their mentor’s supporting email is submitted by the same deadline.
  • All required materials are received on time.

Late applications may not be supported or processed by the Faculty.

How to Apply

Candidates interested in applying through the Faculty of Asian and Middle Eastern Studies at the University of Oxford should submit their application form via email to: amy.richardson@humanities.ox.ac.uk 

Applicants should also arrange for their prospective mentor to submit the required confirmation and assessment to the same email address before the deadline.

Final Thoughts

The British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–27 represent an outstanding opportunity for emerging scholars to accelerate their academic careers through a prestigious three-year fellowship at the University of Oxford. By combining advanced research opportunities, teaching experience, professional mentorship, and access to a globally recognized academic environment, the fellowship provides an ideal pathway for talented researchers seeking to establish themselves as future leaders in the Humanities and Social Sciences.

Eligible candidates are encouraged to begin discussions with potential mentors as early as possible and prepare their application materials well ahead of the deadline to maximize their chances of success.

For more information, visit the official website.

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–27 at the University of Oxford: Fully Funded Three-Year Research Opportunity for Early Career Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ASPIRE Fellows Program 2026: Work with Governments, Scale Evidence-Based Solutions, and Transform Public Policy in India
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Young professionals seeking a meaningful career in development, public policy, governance, and evidence-based social impact now have an exceptional opportunity to work directly with government systems through the ASPIRE Fellows Program hosted by J-PAL South Asia. The ASPIRE Fellowship is a prestigious two-year professional engagement designed to bridge the gap between research and policy implementation. […]

The post ASPIRE Fellows Program 2026: Work with Governments, Scale Evidence-Based Solutions, and Transform Public Policy in India appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young professionals seeking a meaningful career in development, public policy, governance, and evidence-based social impact now have an exceptional opportunity to work directly with government systems through the ASPIRE Fellows Program hosted by J-PAL South Asia.

The ASPIRE Fellowship is a prestigious two-year professional engagement designed to bridge the gap between research and policy implementation. Through this unique initiative, fellows work alongside researchers, government departments, policymakers, and civil society organizations to integrate evidence-based solutions into large-scale government programs across India.

Hosted by J-PAL South Asia, the fellowship provides emerging development professionals with unparalleled exposure to policy implementation, government partnerships, program scaling, monitoring and evaluation, and public sector innovation.

For individuals passionate about translating research into real-world impact, the ASPIRE Fellows Program offers a unique platform to contribute to transformative change while building a strong foundation for a long-term career in the development sector.

What Is the ASPIRE Fellows Program?

The ASPIRE Fellows Program is an immersive professional fellowship that enables young professionals with two to five years of experience to work closely with J-PAL South Asia’s extensive network of:

  • Researchers
  • Government ministries
  • State government departments
  • Development partners
  • Civil society organizations
  • Technical experts
  • Implementation agencies

The fellowship focuses on scaling evidence-based interventions through government systems.

Rather than working solely in research environments, fellows become embedded within government departments and institutions, helping policymakers implement programs backed by rigorous evidence and evaluation.

The program creates a critical connection between research findings and practical implementation, ensuring that effective interventions can be expanded to reach millions of beneficiaries.

About J-PAL South Asia

J-PAL South Asia is part of the global Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab (J-PAL), a leading research center dedicated to reducing poverty through evidence-informed policy.

Through partnerships with governments, researchers, and development organizations, J-PAL promotes the use of rigorous evidence to design, test, and scale programs that improve lives.

The ASPIRE Fellowship represents one of J-PAL South Asia’s key initiatives to strengthen the use of evidence within public systems and government decision-making processes.

Fellowship Duration and Structure

The ASPIRE Fellowship is a full-time, two-year engagement.

Phase 1: Intensive Onboarding at J-PAL South Asia

During the first two months, selected fellows are placed at J-PAL South Asia’s Delhi office.

This onboarding phase includes:

  • Intensive orientation
  • Technical training
  • Exposure to evidence-based policymaking
  • Understanding government partnerships
  • Learning about implementation science
  • Familiarization with program scaling methodologies
  • Professional development and mentorship

This foundation prepares fellows for successful deployment within government systems.

Phase 2: Government Placement

After completing onboarding, fellows are placed within designated government departments and offices according to their assigned projects.

These placements allow fellows to work directly within government structures and contribute to the implementation and scaling of high-impact interventions.

Goals of the ASPIRE Fellows Program

The fellowship is built around two major objectives.

1. Strengthening Collaboration Between Research and Government

The program seeks to foster convergence among:

  • J-PAL South Asia
  • Policymakers
  • Government systems
  • Technical partners
  • Development practitioners

By strengthening these partnerships, the fellowship supports the effective scaling of interventions proven to work through rigorous evidence.

2. Developing Knowledge on Scaling Evidence-Based Programs

The fellowship contributes to building locally grounded knowledge by:

  • Documenting lessons learned
  • Capturing implementation insights
  • Studying challenges in scaling programs
  • Identifying best practices
  • Supporting continuous learning

This knowledge helps improve future development interventions across India.

What Kind of Work Do ASPIRE Fellows Do?

ASPIRE Fellows serve as critical links between research, implementation, and policymaking.

Their responsibilities may include:

Program Management

  • Coordinating large-scale initiatives
  • Supporting implementation activities
  • Managing project timelines
  • Tracking program performance

Stakeholder Engagement

  • Working with government officials
  • Facilitating collaboration among partners
  • Supporting communication across institutions
  • Building relationships with key stakeholders

Technical Assistance

  • Supporting policy implementation
  • Providing evidence-based recommendations
  • Assisting program design and refinement
  • Contributing to strategic planning

Monitoring and Evaluation

  • Supporting data collection efforts
  • Coordinating evaluations
  • Interpreting research findings
  • Translating evidence into actionable recommendations

Organizational Learning

  • Documenting lessons learned
  • Sharing best practices
  • Facilitating knowledge management
  • Strengthening institutional learning systems

Meet the Impact: Examples of Current ASPIRE Fellows

The experiences of current fellows demonstrate the breadth and significance of projects supported through the program.

Sanjana Chettri

Sanjana serves as an ASPIRE Fellow supporting the Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM), a flagship initiative under India’s Ministry of Rural Development.

Her work focuses on:

  • Coordinating learning and knowledge-building activities
  • Supporting the establishment of a Gender Impact Lab
  • Designing evidence-based solutions for rural women
  • Strengthening learning systems within DAY-NRLM

Prior to joining the fellowship, she worked with organizations such as:

  • Oxfam
  • Belongg
  • 60 Decibels

She also led a preventive mental health initiative in partnership with the Government of Delhi.

Nidhi Arun

Nidhi supports innovative education programs implemented through Karnataka’s Department of School Education and Literacy.

Her work involves:

  • Supporting program design
  • Scaling educational interventions
  • Coordinating impact evaluations
  • Building policy evidence

Programs she supports include:

  • ConnectED
  • Maru Sinchana
  • Every Child Counts
  • Gender Equity Curriculum

Before joining the fellowship, she worked with:

  • Indiana University Public Policy Institute
  • Government Economic Service (United Kingdom)
  • International Finance Corporation (IFC)

These examples illustrate the diversity of opportunities available to fellows across sectors including education, gender, livelihoods, and governance.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship is designed for highly motivated professionals who want to contribute to meaningful public sector transformation.

Ideal applicants are individuals who:

  • Want to build a career in development.
  • Have a strong interest in public policy.
  • Enjoy solving complex implementation challenges.
  • Are passionate about social impact.
  • Believe in evidence-based decision-making.
  • Want to work directly with government systems.
  • Seek hands-on experience in scaling interventions.

Skills and Competencies Sought

Successful applicants are expected to demonstrate strong capabilities in the following areas.

Stakeholder Management and Facilitation

Candidates should be able to:

  • Build partnerships
  • Coordinate diverse stakeholders
  • Facilitate discussions
  • Manage relationships effectively

Program Management

Applicants should possess:

  • Project planning skills
  • Organizational abilities
  • Time management capabilities
  • Coordination experience

Technical Assistance

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills are valuable for supporting government programs and policy implementation.

Organizational Learning

Applicants should be interested in generating, documenting, and sharing knowledge to improve future interventions.

Understanding of Monitoring and Evaluation

Knowledge of monitoring and evaluation is considered highly valuable because fellows frequently engage with:

  • Evidence generation
  • Impact assessments
  • Learning systems
  • Program improvement processes

Candidates with M&E experience may be particularly competitive.

Benefits of Becoming an ASPIRE Fellow

The fellowship offers numerous professional advantages.

Professional Development

Fellows receive:

  • Intensive onboarding
  • Practical training
  • Mentorship opportunities
  • Exposure to leading researchers

Government Experience

Participants gain first-hand experience working within government systems and public institutions.

Large-Scale Impact

Fellows contribute to programs that affect millions of people across India.

Evidence-Based Policy Exposure

Participants learn how rigorous research informs policymaking and implementation.

High-Level Networking

Fellows build relationships with:

  • Government leaders
  • Researchers
  • Development experts
  • International organizations
  • Civil society actors

Career Advancement

The fellowship provides exceptional experience for future careers in:

  • Public policy
  • International development
  • Government consulting
  • Monitoring and evaluation
  • Research and implementation
  • Program management

Application Process

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis.

Step 1: Submit an Application

Candidates must submit:

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Cover Letter
  • Short Video Introduction

The video should introduce:

  • The applicant
  • Their background
  • Their motivation for joining the fellowship
  • Their interest in evidence-based development

Step 2: Case Study Assessment

Shortlisted candidates will be invited to complete a case study or designated assignment.

This stage evaluates:

  • Analytical thinking
  • Problem-solving abilities
  • Communication skills
  • Understanding of development challenges

Step 3: Interview Rounds

Successful candidates proceed to two rounds of interviews with the J-PAL South Asia team.

The interviews assess:

  • Technical competencies
  • Professional experience
  • Motivation
  • Alignment with fellowship objectives

Projects and Government Partners

Fellows may be assigned to projects involving:

Ministry of Education

Supporting educational innovation, learning outcomes, and policy implementation.

Ministry of Rural Development

Working on large-scale livelihood, gender, and rural development programs.

Additional opportunities may emerge through J-PAL South Asia’s expanding network of government and development partners.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should prepare:

  1. An updated CV.
  2. A compelling cover letter.
  3. A short introductory video.

Applications are accepted through the ASPIRE Fellows Program platform.

For questions regarding the fellowship or application process, applicants may contact:

  • vasharma@povertyactionlab.org
  • tpande@povertyactionlab.org

Application Deadline

No fixed deadline has been announced. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Interested candidates are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible.

Why the ASPIRE Fellowship Stands Out

Few opportunities allow young professionals to work directly at the intersection of research, government, and large-scale implementation. The ASPIRE Fellows Program provides a rare chance to embed within public systems, influence policy implementation, strengthen evidence use in governance, and contribute to meaningful development outcomes.

For ambitious professionals seeking to build expertise in development, public policy, monitoring and evaluation, and government partnerships, the ASPIRE Fellowship offers an outstanding pathway to accelerate both professional growth and social impact.

Apply here  

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post ASPIRE Fellows Program 2026: Work with Governments, Scale Evidence-Based Solutions, and Transform Public Policy in India appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
